concerns.text: SC-002 states: 'The correlation between covariate shift magnitude and calibration error is measured against the Spearman rank correlation coefficient. (See FR-006)'. However, FR-006 is defined as 'System MUST perform a permutation-based change-point detection analysis...'. The Spearman correlation is not mentioned in FR-006, nor is it explicitly anchored to any other FR. This creates a traceability gap where the success criterion references a functional requirement that does not implement it.

SC-003 states: 'The existence of abrupt shifts is measured against the permutation-based change-point detection algorithm's confidence interval. (See FR-006)'. While FR-006 covers change-point detection, the Success Criteria section implies a specific measurement (confidence interval) that is not explicitly detailed in the FR text. Additionally, SC-002 and SC-003 both anchor to FR-006, but SC-002 (Spearman correlation) is not supported by FR-006's text. This suggests FR-006 is overloaded or SC-002 is mis-anchored.
